operator: HipKittens SharedKV backward latent-dKV epilogue fork

source_change.mode_6_contract: Return early for all-inactive four-warp CTAs, compute a per-warp route_mask_allow_producer from the compact block/tile QueryTileMask, zero P_ij_bf16_col before dV accumulation, zero dP_ij_bf16_col before dK accumulation, and gate final dQ plus latent dKV writes for inactive resident tiles.

active_tiles_0.note: All-inactive block/tile sidecar path still early-returns correctly.

decision: reject mode_6 producer-value culling as a C4 SharedKV speed path
read: Zeroing the P/dP tiles after they have been produced is still too late for the current four-warp HipKittens owner. It avoids inactive latent dKV outputs and validates the compact sidecar plumbing, but any remaining active resident tile keeps the full QK/P/dP/dQ query-sweep producer alive. Mode 6 is slower than the prior mode-5 store/dQ gate for both active_tiles=8 and active_tiles=1.
next_gate: Do not keep adding late masks to this four-warp CTA. The next HipKittens candidate should make compressed-route ownership part of the producer decomposition itself, for example by splitting resident KV tiles into one-warp/tile CTAs or by building a source-boundary owner whose score/prob tile is keyed by active compressed groups before QK/P/dP/dQ accumulation.
